title: ranunculus brutius subsp. brutius
intro: Ranunculus brutius subsp. brutius, a lesser-known species of the Ranunculus genus, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and edible parts. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Ranunculus brutius subsp. brutius include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin conditions, and provide relief from respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
